subject: Computer Networking
title: Encryption in Transit and at Rest
description: A micro-lesson that makes Encryption in Transit and at Rest usable.

hook.text: Your data is vulnerable in two states: while moving across a network and while sitting on a disk.

buildup.text: Encryption in transit uses TLS to protect data as it travels. Nobody between sender and receiver can read it.

discovery.text: Encryption at rest uses AES to protect stored data. A stolen hard drive reveals only encrypted gibberish.

twist.text: Many breaches leak unencrypted backups or databases. Encrypting stored data is often forgotten until it's too late.

climax.text: Full-disk encryption, database encryption, and backup encryption together cover most data-at-rest scenarios.

punchline.text: Encrypt data moving and data sitting. Cover both states.

quiz.question: What does encryption at rest protect against?
options: Data being read from stolen or compromised storage devices
Data being intercepted during network transmission
Unauthorized users logging into the system
